messages.author: OscarMike
messages.text: Is there a way or can it be added to flood ping a repeater node? I need to be able to test connection of a repeater. The only way I know of is to have a password and attempt to sign in via flood. If I could ping (flood) a repeater, I could immediately know if I have connection of that node with my mesh network. Thanks for the help!
messages.content_html: Is there a way or can it be added to flood ping a repeater node? I need to be able to test connection of a repeater. The only way I know of is to have a password and attempt to sign in via flood. If I could ping (flood) a repeater, I could immediately know if I have connection of that node with my mesh network. Thanks for the help!

messages.author: OscarMike
messages.content_html: New feature discovered Thanks.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Ceres Station 🌑
messages.content_html: what was the answer?
messages.reply_html:
OscarMike
New feature discovered Thanks.

messages.author: OscarMike
messages.text: Well, I guess that guys comment got removed. He told me about the Trace feature. But I also am curious, when you go to tools and Discover Repeaters, is there a way to search Flood for repeaters connected to the mesh? If I am traveling, for example, how do I search for a new repeater without waiting for the repeater to auto-advert.
messages.content_html: Well, I guess that guys comment got removed. He told me about the Trace feature. But I also am curious, when you go to tools and Discover Repeaters, is there a way to search Flood for repeaters connected to the mesh? If I am traveling, for example, how do I search for a new repeater without waiting for the repeater to auto-advert.

messages.author: Ceres Station 🌑
messages.text: just send out a flood message
messages.content_html: just send out a flood message

messages.author: Bullit
messages.text: You dont need to „see“ them. They will repeat your Message anyway. If you have them discovered or not.
messages.content_html: You dont need to „see“ them. They will repeat your Message anyway. If you have them discovered or not.
messages.reactions_html: 2
messages.reply_html:
OscarMike
Well, I guess that guys comment got removed. He told me about the Trace feature. But I also am curious, when you go to tools and Discover Repeaters, is there a way to search Flood for repeaters connected to the mesh? If I am traveling, for example, how do I search for a new repeater without waiting for the repeater to auto-advert.

messages.author: OscarMike
messages.text: Thanks. I understand that. MeshCore is still so new to our area that repeaters are far and few between. The handful of LoRa users are still in Meshtastic and are slowly converting over. Our team is building out a regional MeshCore system as a redundancy to cellular and ham radio systems. Primarily for Skywarn and National Weather Service. Just seeing how I can check for connectivity between nodes. Trace feature will work ok, but a flood ping would be better in my opinion.
messages.content_html: Thanks. I understand that. MeshCore is still so new to our area that repeaters are far and few between. The handful of LoRa users are still in Meshtastic and are slowly converting over. Our team is building out a regional MeshCore system as a redundancy to cellular and ham radio systems. Primarily for Skywarn and National Weather Service. Just seeing how I can check for connectivity between nodes. Trace feature will work ok, but a flood ping would be better in my opinion.

messages.author: Ceres Station 🌑
messages.text: hey try to request telemetry from the repeater in question
messages.content_html: hey try to request telemetry from the repeater in question
